# Third Rail Systems OÜ

> EU-native minimum-disclosure compliance architecture for enterprise travel risk. Resolves the ISO 31030 duty-of-care mandate without triggering GDPR Article 9 special-category data liabilities. Built in Tallinn, Estonia.

Third Rail Systems OÜ is a deep-tech compliance company building the architectural pattern that lets multinationals fulfil their ISO 31030 duty-of-care obligations to marginalised employees without collecting the GDPR Article 9 data that turns protection into liability. The founder, Levi Hankins, is a 20-year US Navy veteran. The architecture has been independently validated at IRL 5 (Technology Readiness Level) by the KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm.

## Core pages

- [Homepage](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/): platform overview, architecture, validation, and pilot contact form.
- [The Shadow HR Liability Brief](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/catch-22): long-form analytical brief explaining why every multinational with a diverse workforce is exposed to the same regulatory pattern, the H&M and Hamburg DPA precedents, and the architectural pattern that resolves it.
- [The Strategic Memo](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/memo): founder's operational thesis on minimum-disclosure compliance architecture, KTH IRL 5 validation, and why the EU's catch-22 is solvable from Tallinn.
- [Beyond Disclosure (whitepaper)](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/beyond-disclosure): a European architectural alternative for protecting marginalised populations across workforce and travel contexts. The founder's long-form paper on why disclosure-based protection fails and the four architectural principles that resolve it.
- [Source list and citation library](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/beyond-disclosure/sources): forty verified claims across four research modules supporting the whitepaper, with primary sources, exact quotations, credibility assessments, and documented corrections.
- [Reference library](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/reference): Index of the machine-citable reference pages: special category data, travel risk DPIAs, medication at borders, assistance codes, civil-society casework, and the glossary. Every page carries stable section anchors.
- [Special category data in employee travel](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/special-category-data): what GDPR Article 9 prohibits, what ISO 31030 requires, and the architectural resolution. Question-structured reference with stable anchors and sourced claims.
- [Duty of care for human rights defenders: the file problem](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/civil-society): Duty of care for human rights defenders and field staff: why the protection file is itself the risk, and the architecture for protection without registration. Question-structured, stable anchors, sourced claims.
- [Does travel risk management require a DPIA?](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/travel-risk-dpia): Whether travel risk management requires a DPIA under GDPR Article 35, what the assessment usually finds, and minimisation as the mitigation. Question-structured, stable anchors, sourced claims.
- [Glossary](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/glossary): Definitions of Shadow HR, minimum disclosure, stateless synthesis, target inventory, mandate, and related terms, each citable by stable anchor, with external coinages credited.
- [Prescription medication at borders: the employer problem](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/medication-at-borders): Why lawful prescriptions are a border arrest risk, why asking travellers about medication is an Article 9 problem, and assessment without disclosure as the resolution. Question-structured, stable anchors, sourced claims.
- [What airline assistance codes disclose](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/assistance-codes): What SSR assistance codes (WCHR, WCHS, WCHC) disclose, who sees them across the reservation and PNR ecosystem, and what employers should and should not retain. Question-structured, stable anchors, sourced claims.
- [Diagnostic intake](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/diagnostic): confidential 60-minute architectural diagnostic for security and GRC teams at multinational enterprises. No HRIS integration required.
- [Security and data handling](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/security): transport and application controls, retention posture, RFC 9116 vulnerability disclosure, and explicit non-claims. Every statement verifiable by the reader.

## Documents

- [ISI Architecture Diagram (PDF)](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/assets/isi-architecture-diagram.pdf): the Information Sanitisation Infrastructure layer diagram referenced on the homepage architecture section.
- [KTH IRL Evidence Report (DOCX)](https://thirdrailsystems.ee/assets/kth-irl-evidence-report-third-rail-systems.docx): the KTH Royal Institute of Technology Innovation Readiness Level evidence report cited in the validation section.
